meta.name: Basic Soft White Sandwich Bread
meta.subtitle: Soft loaf for sandwiches + toast (fresh milled or commercial flour)

yield.notes: 1 loaf (8.5x4.5 in / 9x5 in pan), typically 12–16 slices depending on thickness.

ingredients.label: Flour (bread flour or all-purpose; fresh milled wheat flour works best sifted)
ingredients.notes: If using fresh milled flour, see Fresh-Milled Notes for hydration guidance.

ingredients.label: Yeast (instant or active dry)
ingredients.notes: Instant: mix with flour. Active dry: bloom in warm liquid with sugar 5–10 minutes.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil or butter (to grease pan)

steps.title: Prep + yeast choice
instructions: Warm the water and milk to 90–105°F (32–40°C).
If using active dry yeast: stir yeast + a pinch of the sugar into the warm liquids and let stand 5–10 minutes until foamy.
If using instant yeast: do not bloom; add it with the dry ingredients in the next step.

steps.title: Mix
instructions: In a bowl (or stand mixer bowl), combine flour, sugar, and salt.
Add yeast now if using instant.
Add warm liquids and the egg.
Mix until a shaggy dough forms, scraping down the bowl as needed.

steps.title: Rest (hydrate)
instructions: Let the dough rest 10 minutes to hydrate the flour and make kneading easier.

steps.title: Knead + incorporate butter
instructions: Knead until the dough begins to smooth (mixer medium-low 4–6 minutes; by hand 8–10 minutes).
Add softened butter in small pieces and continue kneading until fully incorporated and the dough is elastic and slightly tacky (mixer 3–5 minutes; by hand 5–8 minutes).
The dough should pass a light windowpane test: stretch a small piece— it should thin without tearing immediately.

steps.title: First rise (bulk fermentation)
instructions: Lightly oil a bowl, place dough inside, cover.
Rise until doubled (about 60–90 minutes depending on room temperature).

steps.title: Shape
instructions: Grease a loaf pan.
Turn dough out and gently press into a rectangle about the width of the pan.
Roll into a tight log, pinch seam closed, and place seam-side down in the pan.

steps.title: Second rise (proof)
instructions: Cover and proof until the dough crowns about 1 inch (2–3 cm) above the rim of the pan.
Typical proof time is 45–75 minutes depending on temperature.

steps.title: Bake
instructions: Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 28–35 minutes until deep golden brown.
If browning too fast, tent loosely with foil for the last 10 minutes.
Optional doneness check: internal temperature ~195–205°F (90–96°C).

steps.title: Cool + slice
instructions: Remove from pan and cool on a rack at least 60 minutes before slicing (90 minutes gives the best crumb).
Optional: brush warm crust with butter for a softer top.

freshMilledNotes.summary: Fresh milled flour often absorbs more water and benefits from sifting for a softer loaf.
guidelines: Start with the listed liquids but hold back 20–30g water during mixing; add only if the dough feels stiff/dry.
For maximum softness, sift/bolt fresh-milled flour to remove some bran; fully unsifted whole wheat will bake up denser.
Consider extending the first rest (autolyse) to 15–20 minutes for fresh milled flour.

storage.counter: Cool completely, then store tightly wrapped at room temperature 2–3 days.
storage.freezer: Slice and freeze in a bag up to 2–3 months. Toast slices directly from frozen for best texture.
storage.notes: Avoid refrigerating; it stales bread faster.

variations.name: Milkier loaf (softer)
variations.change: Replace 60g water with 60g more milk (total milk 120g).

variations.name: Honey loaf
variations.change: Replace sugar with 30g honey; reduce water by 10g.

variations.name: Tangzhong option (extra-soft, longer-lasting)
variations.change: Cook 25g flour + 125g water to a paste (150°F/65°C), cool, then subtract those amounts from the main flour/water.
variations.notes: Produces a pillowy loaf with improved shelf life.

notes: If your kitchen is cold, proof in an unheated oven with the light on, or near a warm appliance.
If the loaf collapses at the top, it likely over-proofed—reduce proof time next bake.

audit.testNotes: Canonical baseline loaf designed for soft sandwich slices and toast; compatible with fresh milled or commercial flour.
